reasoning: The window between the May 20, 2025 knowledge cutoff and the July 1, 2025 resolution date is only 41 days. This 3-month gap between the political statement and the current status implies that there is no rapid PR-driven initiative to scrub the phrase from the website. Actually changing SNAP eligible foods typically involves Congress (via a Farm Bill) or a lengthy administrative rule/waiver process. Even if such processes are underway, finalizing them and updating the primary consumer-facing FNS webpage by July 1 is relatively unlikely in such a short timeframe. However, anticipating a small risk of a unilateral, optics-driven edit by the administration, I reduce the probability slightly from a near-certain base rate. I estimate the probability of the phrase remaining at 83%.
